reporter_question: How are you feeling after beating a top-20 player on Centre Court?
quote: I've never felt emotions like this before. I just want to keep doing the same thing — stay composed and trust my game.

reporter_question: Were you nervous walking out for the quarter-final?
quote: Of course I was nervous, but once the match started I felt calm. The crowd helped me settle.

reporter_question: How would you rate your performance today?
quote: It was almost a perfect match. I served well, stayed aggressive, and trusted myself from the first point.

reporter_question: Arthur Fery will have the Centre Court crowd on Friday — does that bother you?
quote: Not at all. I've played big matches with crowds against me. I focus on my tennis, not the noise.

reporter_question: Five hours and fifteen minutes — how do you feel physically?
quote: It was the longest match of the tournament for me. I had a small calf issue in the first set but I won with my racquet and my heart.

reporter_question: Can you recover in time for the semi-final?
quote: I have two days to recover. I've been in this situation many times at Wimbledon. The body knows what to do.

reporter_question: What went wrong today against Zverev?
quote: I wasn't fully there mentally. The knee was bothering me and I couldn't find my rhythm on serve.
stats_note: Context for Zverev dominance — not Zverev quote

reporter_question: Another straight-sets win — how is your level right now?
quote: I'm feeling good. I'm moving well and not giving away cheap points. That's the plan for the semis.
